prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='DWT_rf', seasonality_champion='NaiveSeasonal', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.76) and very high seasonality variance (0.98), indicating models capture patterns very differently. DWT_rf dominates as overall champion with best trend tracking, while NaiveSeasonal excels at seasonality. Early/late horizon specialists differ significantly, supporting per-horizon selection combined with DBA for handling the high disagreement.

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "DWT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "NaiveSeasonal",
  "overall_champion": "DWT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ETS", "late": "DWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["DWT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "THETA", "ETS", "DWT_catboost", "CWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["FT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "FT_catboost", "ARIMA", "ONLY_FT_rf", "FT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.7602,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.76) and very high seasonality variance (0.98), indicating models capture patterns very differently. DWT_rf dominates as overall champion with best trend tracking, while NaiveSeasonal excels at seasonality. Early/late horizon specialists differ significantly, supporting per-horizon selection combined with DBA for handling the high disagreement."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "dba_combination"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection. RMSE spread_ratio=0.76 suggests high model dispersion favoring top-k with shrinkage. n_windows=3 (very few) requires conservative top_k=3 and shrinkage=0.25 to prevent overfitting. DBA added as recommended by pattern_analyst_insights for handling phase-shifted predictions. best_per_horizon captures early/late horizon specialists from validation."
}
